questions.question_text: The calendar of year 1856 is same as which year?
questions.explanation_text:
Given year 1856, when divided by 4 leaves a remainder of 0.
NOTE: When the remainder is 0, 28 is added to the given year to get the result.
So, 1856 + 28 = 1884

questions.question_text: How many leap years do 100 years have?
questions.explanation_text:
The given year is divided by 4, and the quotient gives the number of leap years.
Here, 100 {tex}\div{/tex} 4 = 25.
But, as 100 is not a leap year {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 25 - 1= 24 leap years.

questions.question_text: On 8th Feb, 2005 it was Tuesday. What was the day of the week on 8th Feb, 2004?
questions.explanation_text:
The year 2004 is a leap year. It has 2 odd days.
The day on 8th Feb 2004 is 2 days before the day on 8th Feb 2005.
Hence, this day is Sunday.

questions.question_text: On 1st Jan, 2005 Saturday falls. What day of the week was it on 1st Jan, 2006?
questions.explanation_text:
The year 1985 is an ordinary year. So, it has 1 odd day.
So, the day on 19th June 1985 will be 1 day after the day on 19th June 1984.
But, 19th June 1984 is Monday.
S0, 19th June 1985 is Tuesday.

questions.question_text: What was the day of the week on 16th August 1947?
questions.explanation_text:
15th August, 1947 = (1946 years + Period from 1st Jan, 1947 to 15th)
Counting of odd days:
1600 years have 0 odd day. 300 years have 1 odd day.
47 years = (11 leap years + 36 ordinary years) = [(11 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) + (36 {tex}\times{/tex} 1) ]odd days = 58 odd days = 2 odd days
Jan, Feb, Mar, Apr, May, Jun, Jul, Aug.
31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 15 = 227 days = (32 weeks + 3 days) = 3
Total number of odd days = (0 + 1 + 2 + 3) odd days = 6 odd days
Hence, the required day was 'Saturday'.

questions.question_text: What was the day of the week on 17th June, 1998?
questions.explanation_text:
17th June, 1998 = (1997 years + Period from 1.1.1998 to 17.6.1998)
Odd days in 1600 years = 0
Odd days in 300 years = (5 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) 1
97 years has 24 leap years + 73 ordinary years.
Number of odd days in 97 years (24 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 73) = 121 = 2 odd days.
Jan, Feb, March, April, May, June
(31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 17) = 168 days
168 days = 24 weeks = 0 odd day
Total number of odd days = (0 + 1 + 2 + 0) = 3.
Given day is Wednesday

questions.question_text: The calendar for the year 2018 will be the same for the year:
questions.explanation_text:
How to find the years which have the same Calendars:
Leap year calendar repeats every 28 years.
Here 28 is distributed as 6 + 11 + 11
Rules:
If given year is at 1st position after Leap year then next repeated calendar year is Given Year + 6.
If given year is at 2nd position after Leap year then next repeated calendar year is Given Year + 11.
If given year is at 3rd position after Leap year then next repeated calendar year is Given Year + 11.
Now, the given year is 2018.
We know that 2016 is a Leap year.
2016 - LY; 2017 - 1st; 2018 - 2nd; 2019 - 3rd; 2020 - LY
Here 2018 is at 2nd position after the Leap year.
According to rule (b) the calendar of 2018 is repeated for the year is 2018 + 11 = 2029.

questions.question_text: What was the day on 25th Jan, 1981?
questions.explanation_text:
25 Jan, 1981 = (1980 years + Period from 1.1.1981 to 25.1.1981)
Odd days in 1600 years = 0
Odd days in 300 years = 1
80 years = (60 ordinary years + 20 leap year) = (60 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) = 2 odd days
Jan = 25 days = (3 weeks + 4 days) = 4 odd days
Total number of odd days = (0 + 1 + 2 + 4) = 7= 0 odd day
The given day is Sunday.

questions.question_text: The calendar of the year 1897 can be used again in the year:
questions.explanation_text:
Repetition of leap year
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Add +28 to the Given Year.
Repetition of a non-leap year
Step 1: Add +11 to the Given Year. If Result is a leap year, Go to step 2.
Step 2: Add +6 to the Given Year.
Solution:
The given Year is 1897, Which is a non-leap year.
Step 1: Add +11 to the given year (i.e 1897 + 11) = 1908, Which is a leap year.
Step 2: Add +6 to the given year (i.e 1897 + 6) = 1903
Therefore, The calendar of the year 1897 can be used again in the year 1903

questions.question_text: What was the day on 9th May 2001?
questions.explanation_text:
9th May, 2001 = (2000 years + Period from 1.1.2001 to 9.5.2001)
Odd days in 1600 years = 0
Odd days in 400 years = 0
Jan. Feb. March April May
(31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 9) = 129 days
129 days = (18 weeks + 3 day) = 3 odd day
Total number of odd days = (0 + 0 + 3 ) = 3 odd day
Given day is Wednesday.

questions.question_text: The second & fourth Saturdays and every Sunday is a holiday. How many working days will be there in a month of 31 days beginning on a Friday?
questions.explanation_text:
Given that the month begins on a Friday and has 31 days
Sundays = 3rd, 10th, 17th, 24th, 31st
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Total Sundays = 5
Every second & fourth Saturday is a holiday.
2nd & 4th Saturday in every month = 2
Total days in the month = 31
Total working days = 31 - (5 + 2) = 24 days.

questions.question_text: What was the day on 2nd Jan 1901?
questions.explanation_text:
2nd Jan 1901 means
(1900 years and 2 days)
Now, 1600 years have 0 odd day
300 years have 1 odd day
2 days have 2 odd day
Total no. of odd days = 0 + 1 + 2 = 3 days
Hence, the day on 2nd Jan 1901 was Wednesday.

questions.question_text: On 24th Nov 2007 Thursday falls. What day of the week was it on 10th Nov 2006?
questions.explanation_text:
The year 2006 is an ordinary year. So, it has 1 odd day.
So, the day on 24th Nov, 2007 will be 1 day beyond the day on 24th Nov, 2006.
But, 24th Nov, 2007 is Thursday.
24 - 10 = 14 days.
Therefore, 2 weeks ago it is same day.
Thus, 10th Nov, 2006 is one day before 10th Nov, 2007 i.e. it is Wednesday.

questions.question_text: 1.12.91 is the first Sunday. Which is the fourth Tuesday of December 91?
questions.explanation_text:
1/12/91 is the first Sunday of December 91
So, 3/12/91 is the first Tuesday of the month.
Clearly, 10/12/91, 17/12/91, 24/12/91, and 31/12/91 are also Tuesdays.
So, 24/12/91 is the fourth Tuesday.

questions.question_text: How many leap years do 300 years have?
questions.explanation_text:
The given year is divided by 4, and the quotient gives the number of leap years.
Here, 300 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 75.
But, as 100, 200 and 300 are not in leap years {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 75 - 3 = 72 leap years.
